arabic_text:
وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلَدَكُم مِّنْ إِمْلَقٍ ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُكُمْ وَإِيَّاهُمْ
وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلَدَكُمْ خَشْيَةَ إِمْلَقٍ ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُهُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ
english_text:
(6:151) Do not kill your children because of poverty — we provide for you all (parents) and them (children)
(17:31) Do not kill your children out of fear of poverty — we provide for them (children) and you all (parents)
alt_text: Side-by-side comparison of two Quran verses (6:151 and 17:31) with annotated translations, showing the subtle inversion: '…because of poverty / we provide for you and them' vs. '…out of fear of poverty / we provide for them and you'.
notes: Quran 6:151 vs 17:31 — classical exemplar of Quranic precision: when poverty is present (6:151) parents are mentioned first; when fear of future poverty is anticipated (17:31) children are mentioned first. Citation marks both verses.
